title: Functions, Transformations, Inverses, and Composition · Model Lens: Multi Representation
summary: Model sensor calibration and reversible conversions across representations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int multi representation increment in a calibration and conversion library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.
Compose functions while checking intermediate domain and unit compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Multi Representation evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (1, 5) and (5, 17). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Multi Representation for a calibration and conversion library.
steps.why: The Multi Representation result for a calibration and conversion library is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.
